context: Verify that the agent removes non-null assertions and replaces them with explicit narrowing guards, choosing appropriate default/error strategies.

checklist.name: All `!` non-null assertions removed
checklist.description: The output file contains zero non-null assertion operators (`!`) on optional property accesses.

checklist.name: Each function has an explicit undefined guard
checklist.description: Every function contains a conditional check (`if (x === undefined)`, nullish coalescing, or optional chaining with a fallback) before accessing the potentially undefined value.

checklist.name: Guard strategy is appropriate per function
checklist.description: Functions that can reasonably return a default value use a default (e.g., `?? 0`); functions where absence is an error throw a `TypeError` or `RangeError`.

checklist.name: Inline comments justify each strategy
checklist.description: Each function has an inline comment explaining why the chosen strategy (default vs. throw) was used.

checklist.name: No `any`, `@ts-ignore`, or new `as` assertions introduced
checklist.description: The fix does not introduce any unsafe workarounds that would suppress errors rather than resolve them.
